heroDesc: Non-metal materials for lightweight, insulation, low friction, chemical resistance or appearance prototypes.
bodyDesc: Engineering plastics are useful for weight reduction, insulation, wear behavior and fast validation. POM is dimensionally stable with low friction, PA is tough but moisture absorption matters, PC has impact resistance, and PEEK improves heat and chemical resistance at a higher cost.

panels.title: Suitable processes
panels.subtitle: CNC / Sheet metal / Forming
panels.body: The route should be selected by grade, geometry and order quantity.

panels.title: Required inputs
panels.subtitle: CAD + drawing + material notes
panels.body: Grade, condition, finishing and acceptance requirements should be stated clearly.

panels.title: Risk control
panels.subtitle: Review before production
panels.body: Thin walls, deep cavities, welding and cosmetic requirements should be checked early.

tableRows: Low friction and good dimensional stability
Good toughness; moisture-related size change matters
Higher cost; suitable for demanding conditions

asideItems.label: Typical uses
asideItems.value: Insulation, low friction, lightweight parts and functional prototypes.

asideCta.title: Need material advice?
asideCta.body: Share the working condition, load, appearance and cost targets so engineering can compare suitable materials.

related.title: Related Materials
related.desc: Similar materials differ in strength, weight, corrosion resistance, cost and finishing compatibility, so they are worth comparing before quoting.

items.title: Aluminum Alloys
items.body: Low density and stable machinability for housings, brackets, fixtures and lightweight structures.

items.title: Stainless Steel
items.body: Good corrosion resistance and durability for equipment parts, shafts, fluid-contact and clean environments.

items.title: Carbon Steel
items.body: Balanced strength and cost for load structures, bases, shafts and mechanical connectors.

faq.title: Engineering Plastics FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Engineering Plastics suitable for?
items.a: Engineering Plastics is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Engineering Plastics quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Engineering Plastics?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
